title: Intent Lint
problem: Imprezia's pitch is "one line integration, ad inside the LLM response, intent matched to advertiser." But every AI app developer who's been at this for 2 weeks knows the real question is: "If I let this network insert brand mentions into my product's voice, how do I prevent (a) hallucinated facts about the brand, (b) tonal mismatch with my product, (c) policy violations for regulated verticals (healthcare, finance, kids), and (d) latency tax that breaks streaming?
direction: An open developer toolkit + benchmark that lets an AI app dev simulate, audit, and gate Imprezia style intent ad insertions before they ship — turning "trust the network" into "trust and verify in CI."
why: The Core Systems JD's own language - "throughput, correctness, and failure modes" - is the server side of this work. intent lint is the missing client side + open benchmark. Shipping it is the loudest possible signal of fit.

archetypes.name: imprezia evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: imprezia signal changes while pitch context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: pitch boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: pitch handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: integration regression harness
archetypes.trigger: integration behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: inside operator packet
archetypes.trigger: inside output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
